rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM ROY HORSTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order (1) Barring Deposition Of Respondents Attorney, (2) Excusing Respondent Lidstrom From Answering Interrogatories, Producing Documents And Admitting Facts, And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Lasse Lidstrom's Motion for Protective Order (1) Barring Deposition of Respondent's Attorney and (2) Excusing Lidstrom from Answering Interrogatories, Producing Documents, and Admitting Facts, and for Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion for protective order barring William Matheson's deposition is granted. Albertson and Davidson ("A&D") has not shown that it cannot reasonably obtain the information it seeks from other means or that the information sought from Mr. Matheson is crucial to the case. (Sprectra-Physics v. Superior Court (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 1487, 1496.) Lidstrom has withdrawn his request for an order excusing him from responding to written discovery. Both requests for sanctions are denied. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than January 5, 2016, at 4:00 p.m.
